prompt: Over-the-shoulder photographic view, looking past a homeowner's shoulder toward a contractor in a flannel shirt and worn jeans standing on the front porch steps of a 1980s New England colonial. The contractor gestures with one hand toward a clipboard the homeowner is holding, the other hand resting on a wooden porch column. The two figures are mid-conversation, body language relaxed but focused. Brick steps and a black painted front door visible. Shot with a 35mm prime lens, shallow depth of field — the contractor sharp, the homeowner's shoulder soft in foreground. Late afternoon warm golden side-light catches the contractor's face from camera-right. Slightly desaturated palette, a red coffee thermos on the porch rail as the only saturated color. Working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright, NOT staged. No readable text, no logos.
